papers.title: Runaway OB Stars in the Small Magellanic Cloud III. Updated Kinematics and Insights on Dynamical vs. Supernova Ejections
papers.author_names: Grant D. Phillips M.S. Oey Maria Cuevas Norberto Castro Rishi Kothari

abstract.text: We use the kinematics of field OB stars to estimate the frequencies of runaway stars generated by the dynamical ejection scenario (DES), the binary supernova scenario (BSS), and the combined two-step mechanism. We update the proper motions for field OB and OBe stars in the Small Magellanic Cloud (SMC) using Gaia DR3. Our sample now contains 336 stars from the Runaways and Isolated O-Type Star Spectroscopic Survey of the SMC (RIOTS4), and we update our algorithm to calculate more accurate velocities compared to those obtained previously from DR2. We find a decrease in median velocity from 39 to 29 km/s, implying that the proper motions from our previous work were systematically overestimated. We present the velocity distribution for OBe stars and quantitatively compare it to those of non-compact binaries and high-mass X-ray binaries. We confirm that OBe stars appear to be dominated by the BSS and are likely post-SN binary systems, further supporting the mass-transfer model to explain the origin of their emission-line disks. In contrast, normal OB stars may show a bimodal velocity distribution, as may be expected from different processes that occur with dynamical ejections. The kinematics of fast-rotating OB stars are similar to those of normal OB stars rather than OBe stars, suggesting that the origin of their high v_r*sin(i) is different from that of OBe stars. We update our model parameters describing the kinematic origins of the SMC field population, still confirming that for runaway stars, the DES mechanism dominates, and two-step ejections seem comparable in frequency to pure BSS ejections.

triage.reason: Weak title match: runaway-related mechanisms LLM confirmed: Directly studies runaway OB stars, their kinematics, and ejection mechanisms (DES vs BSS) in SMC

source_fields.sections_text: Methods: The paper proposes a method to identify dynamically ejected stars (DES) and binary systems accelerated by supernovae (BSS) by analyzing proper motions, stellar masses, and binary classifications in a sample of 336 OB field stars from the RIOTS4 survey, using the core idea that non-compact binaries result from dynamical ejection while post-supernova systems like HMXBs and OBe stars with binary characteristics originate from supernova-driven binary evolution. Conclusion: This study confirms that dynamically ejected stars (DES) dominate over supernova ejecta (BSS) in producing high-velocity OB stars in the Small Magellanic Cloud, with improved kinematic data supporting a bimodal velocity distribution and binary ejection mechanisms. Introduction: This paper presents a refined analysis of the dynamical versus binary supernova ejection mechanisms for massive field stars in the SMC using Gaia DR3 proper motions, establishing that dynamical ejections dominate by a factor of 2–3 and revealing that binary supernova runaways are primarily composed of two-step ejections, including fast-rotating OBe and non-OBe stars. Des Vs Bss Ejections: The kinematic data support interpreting OB and OBe stars as primarily originating from DES and BSS ejections, respectively, with observed DES/BSS and walkaway-to-runaway ratios reasonably matched by updated models that incorporate revised ejection fractions and velocities. Updated Gaia Dr3 Kinematics: The updated Gaia DR3 kinematics reveal a significant reduction in median transverse velocities—down by ~35% compared to DR2—due to improved astrometric precision, leading to a more accurate identification of runaways and walkaways and a diminished high-velocity tail, with revised velocities for individual objects such as SMC X-1.

title: Runaway OB Stars in the Small Magellanic Cloud III.

catalog_assessment.evidence: Paper analyzes 336 stars from RIOTS4 survey with updated Gaia DR3 proper motions, presents velocity distributions and candidate classifications
